steps.title: Spot the defining clue
steps.content: The fine grains show “episodic desiccation”—periodic, not permanent drying. This directly constrains what happened in the ancient environment.

steps.title: Match the evidence to the scenario
steps.content:
Choice A describes a lake for a prolonged period with occasional drying and periodic streams—the prolonged lake explains the fine-grain dominance, the occasional drying matches the desiccation cracks in those grains, and the periodic streams account for the coarse-grain intervals.

The other three choices either deny the lake existed, claim the lake never dried despite the evidence, or describe permanent rather than episodic drying.

That matches choice A — A is correct.
